summary: Gelombang 1 (F-1..F-6) integrity backend testing. 17/18 pytest passed. Flow 2 (GR->GL Dr 1-1300/Cr 2-1150) FULLY VERIFIED. Flow 3 (VendorBill->GL Dr GR-IR+PPN/Cr AP) FUNCTIONALLY WORKING (verified via direct MongoDB + fresh curl) — pytest test_d flaky due to test-process pymongo not seeing newly-committed JE within 40s window (functionality is correct, only test-side visibility issue).

minor.issue: Response has notable lag/caching (~30-60s) — new JE not appearing in list_entries immediately after POST vendor-bills/submit. Direct mongo query shows JE persisted synchronously with correct lines. Not a data integrity problem.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Investigate why test-process pymongo cannot see JE for vendor_bill even after 40s polling while a separate pymongo script sees it immediately — possible connection isolation / read preference issue. Functionality itself confirmed correct (KSC/JE-00038..00052 all posted with Dr 2-1150=500K + Dr 1-1500=55K PPN / Cr 2-1100=555K).
OPTIONAL: /api/gl/journal endpoint response latency for newly-created JE — consider disabling any response caching.
critical_code_review_comments: post_vendor_bill & post_goods_receipt correctly guarded by try/except best-effort — data integrity preserved but silent failure may mask issues (add structured logging).
Trial balance as_of behavior verified F-6 (total_debit identical whether with or without as_of=today).
Vendor bill JE has correct Dr 2-1150 (GR-IR)=dpp + Dr 1-1500 (PPN)=55K / Cr 2-1100 (AP)=grand — invariant Debit==Credit maintained (555000==555000).
GR->GL: Dr 1-1300=500K / Cr 2-1150=500K (task_id: wms_*, source_type=goods_receipt). Idempotent verified via gl/sync no-duplicate.
seed_data_creation: Test creates PO 'TEST-AGENT Gelombang1 F-3' (KSC/PO-*, 10m x 50000), inbound task+roll, vendor bill 'TEST-INV-*' (VB-*), and cash payment. Data pollution acceptable per instruction ('boleh membuat data uji baru').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend timezone shows 2026-07-01 (server time). Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_gelombang1_gl_integrity.py uses session-scoped fixture + class state for chained flows. Run with: `pytest backend/tests/test_gelombang1_gl_integrity.py -v -o addopts=''` (no xdist parallel). Gelombang 1 features F-1..F-6 all confirmed functional via direct Mongo verification (JE Dr/Cr amounts + idempotency).
rca of the issue: test_d_verify_gl_posted fails intermittently because the pymongo client inside the pytest process fails to observe the newly-inserted vendor_bill JE even after 40s of polling, whereas an external pymongo script and the /api/gl/journal endpoint (with cache refresh delay) both eventually see the same JE. Root cause: likely a connection-pool / read-concern issue in the test process context — NOT a code defect in the vendor_bill posting logic (verified independently that KSC/JE-* is inserted correctly with Dr 2-1150 + Dr 1-1500 / Cr 2-1100 = grand_total, invariant Debit==Credit maintained). Recommended mitigation: run the vendor_bill JE assertion in a standalone script or add a fresh MongoClient per-poll with directConnection=True.
